evidence_index.verbatim_quote: At around 4:20 p.m. on October 26, 2015, 32-year-old Jonathan Buckelew arrived by ambulance to the emergency department of North Fulton Hospital after he experienced seizure-like activity and became unresponsive during a chiropractic neck adjustment.
evidence_index.summary: Patient arrived at ED after seizure-like activity during chiropractic adjustment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Although the hospital was a primary stroke center, Buckelew’s brain stem stroke was not diagnosed until the following day, after the window of opportunity for a mechanical thrombectomy had passed.
evidence_index.summary: Stroke diagnosis delayed beyond treatment window.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: As a result of this delay in treatment, Buckelew has locked-in syndrome, requiring constant care.
evidence_index.summary: Delay in treatment resulted in locked-in syndrome.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: jury found Dr. Womack and the radiologist liable and awarded Buckelew $75 million in damages.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Womack and radiologist found liable; $75 million awarded.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The jury returned a verdict in favor of the chiropractor, NFMC, the on-call neurologist, and PA Nickum, but found that Dr. Womack and the radiologist were grossly negligent.
evidence_index.summary: Verdict in favor of chiropractor and others; Womack and radiologist grossly negligent.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Lutz opined with a reasonable degree of medical certainty that Dr. Womack grossly departed from the standard of care by failing to inform Dr. Futrell.
evidence_index.summary: Expert opined Womack grossly departed from standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Womack ordered a CT scan and CT angiogram (“CTA”) scan of Buckelew’s head and neck.
evidence_index.summary: CT and CTA scans ordered by Dr. Womack.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Although Dr. Womack listed stroke as a possible diagnosis on the differential diagnosis, he suspected Buckelew was experiencing meningitis or encephalitis.
evidence_index.summary: Stroke listed as possible diagnosis; meningitis or encephalitis suspected.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Womack did not mention that Buckelew had seen a chiropractor, that Buckelew experienced a second seizure-like event while Dr. Womack was performing a lumbar puncture.
evidence_index.summary: Womack did not mention chiropractic visit or second seizure-like event.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The trial court did not err in denying Womack’s motion for directed verdict.
evidence_index.summary: Court denied Womack's motion for directed verdict.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

verdict_detected.key_phrase: jury found Dr. Womack and the radiologist liable

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient experienced seizure-like activity and became unresponsive during a chiropractic neck adjustment.

requestables.name: CT Scan
requestables.description: CT scan of head and neck

requestables.name: CT Angiogram
requestables.description: CT angiogram of head and neck

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with seizure-like activity and possible stroke
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.clinical_reasoning: MRI is more sensitive for detecting acute ischemic stroke.
Lumbar puncture is not indicated without ruling out stroke.

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established MRI is standard for suspected stroke

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on expert testimony and standard of care
explanation.what_should_have_happened: MRI should have been prioritized to confirm stroke

decision_points.prompt: What should be communicated to the on-call neurologist?

options.description: Inform neurologist of chiropractic visit and seizure-like events
options.clinical_reasoning: Complete information is crucial for accurate diagnosis and management.

options.description: Do not mention chiropractic visit or seizure-like events
options.clinical_reasoning: Incomplete information can lead to misdiagnosis.

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not mention chiropractic visit or seizure-like events

expected_action_court.description: Complete information should have been communicated
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Standard practice to provide full clinical picture

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to provide complete information led to delayed diagnosis
explanation.what_should_have_happened: All relevant clinical events should have been communicated

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with acute symptoms

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed locked-in syndrome requiring constant care

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to diagnose stroke timely
Causation established - delay led to locked-in syndrome

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely diagnosis could have prevented locked-in syndrome

court_findings.finding: Dr. Womack and radiologist grossly negligent
court_findings.legal_significance: Breach of duty and causation established
